projectDescription: A Raspberry Pi-based HTTP service that lets you remotely control your desktop's power button from anywhere via a web interface or REST API.
keyFeatures: Remote power button control via HTTP REST API
Web UI with a clickable power button interface
Raspberry Pi GPIO control using go-rpio
Basic HTTP authentication with failed access logging
SQLite-backed press and state history with cursor-based pagination
Dummy backend for local development without hardware
Configurable GPIO input/output pins and press timeout
publicApiSurface: GET /status — current power state, press state, and last button press
POST /press — simulate pressing the power button (optional duration via ?t=)
POST /release — release the power button early
POST /turn/{state} — turn the desktop on or off explicitly
GET /press/history — paginated history of button presses
